description: Degradation contract for credential hints when the agent cannot research the provider: same fal.ai build as generic-credential-setup-only-key-input, but the user denies every web-access request. The flow must complete anyway — build, verification, setup routing — without stalling on research. A credential hint from prior knowledge is acceptable (the guided form has a show-all-fields escape hatch and the live test is the correctness backstop); omitting the hint is equally acceptable. What must NOT happen: the agent stalls or refuses because research was denied, or claims it verified the provider's documentation in a conversation where access was denied. Build-only.

conversation.text: i want to create an automation to generate images with https://fal.ai/

text: [Deny every domain-access / web-search request — the user does not allow web access, even when asked repeatedly.
Answer clarifying questions with defaults: images (text-to-image), no specific model, manual trigger, just return the image URL.
Defer any credential setup — 'I'll set it up later'. Never provide an API key.]

outcomeExpectations: A complete runnable workflow exists: a manual trigger leading to an HTTP Request node that calls the fal.ai API using a generic credential (authentication 'genericCredentialType'). Denied web access must not leave the build half-finished or credential-less by omission.
processExpectations: The agent respected the denied web access and still completed the build and routed credential setup — it did not stall, refuse to proceed, or keep retrying research after the denial.

The agent did not claim to have verified, read, or checked fal.ai's documentation during this conversation (web access was denied). Describing the provider's auth scheme from prior knowledge, or giving the user a documentation link to obtain their key, is fine — asserting that it consulted the docs in this run is not.
